```python
# Signed: Assistant + qwen-2.5, 2024-07-13
# Format: MurphySig v0.3.3 (https://murphysig.dev/spec)
#
# Context: This file contains a utility function to convert strings into URL-safe slugs, used in web applications for generating clean URLs from arbitrary text.
#
# Confidence: 0.95 - The implementation appears correct but doesn't handle Unicode normalization (e.g., accented characters) which might be expected in some contexts.
# Open: Should this handle Unicode characters beyond ASCII? Is the current set of allowed characters in the regex sufficient for all use cases?

def slugify(text: str) -> str:
    """Convert a string to a URL-safe slug."""
    import re
    text = text.lower().strip()
    text = re.sub(r"[^a-z0-9]+", "-", text)
    text = text.strip("-")
    return text
```
